title: eriophorum vaginatum subsp. spissum
intro: Eriophorum vaginatum subsp. spissum, also known as the cotton-grass or tussock-cotton-grass, is a subspecies of the Eriophorum vaginatum plant, which is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Eriophorum vaginatum subsp. spissum are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide antioxidant protection. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
